data.output:
# Story 10 Final Plan — Auto-by-default Escalation + Always-Review

Reconciled with codex review (/tmp/story-10-codex.out). Major shifts:
- All escalation expressed as **MinPower** bumps via Fizeau; no `Profile`/harness/model selection in DDx.
- Reviewer pairing requires a **Fizeau contract extension** (`exclude_provider_identity` / `exclude_provider_class` + power floor); Story 10 blocks on it.
- Decomposition ordering: cheap Step 0 first, then MinPower escalation for substantive failures, then re-decompose if evidence is scope/ambiguity (not capability).
- Cost cap must include reviewer cost; reviewer must report `CostUSD`.
- `--no-review` becomes break-glass with `--no-review-i-know-what-im-doing` ack flag.
- Review must NOT rewrite the attempt commit chain — explicit AC.

## Per-requirement plan

### R1 — Automatic by default
- `ddx work` / `ddx try` / `ddx run` with no flags → Fizeau receives `MinPower=tierFloor(InferTier(b))`, `MaxPower=0` (unconstrained), no harness/model/provider.
- Loop escalates `MinPower` on substantive failure, defers on infrastructure failure.

### R2 — Decomposition
- Keep prompt-driven Step 0 (already shipped).
- Heighten Step 0 hint when `len(desc+ac) > 8000` bytes.
- Cap decomposition depth at 2 (`decomposed-from:<id>` label chain).
- Order: decompose-first only when bead overflows scope heuristics or returns `declined_needs_decomposition`. Otherwise escalate MinPower; only after smart-tier capability failures, re-evaluate decomposition.

### R3 — Cost-tier escalation ladder (MinPower-only)
- Three policy labels (cheap/standard/smart) map to MinPower floors (operator-tunable in catalog YAML, e.g. 10/30/50).
- Substantive failure → ladder.Next bumps MinPower to next floor. Smart-tier failure → bump within smart up to top-power.
- Infrastructure failure → defer with retry-after, no MinPower change.
- Skip-tier rule lives in Fizeau (no viable provider at MinPower → returns typed error → loop bumps to next floor).
- DDx never names providers/models in the escalation logic.

### R4 — Different provider + more powerful reviewer
- **Blocked** on Fizeau contract extension: `ExecuteRequest` needs `ExcludeProvider` (identity or class) + `MinPower` floor for review.
- Algorithm: review request = `{MinPower: max(impl_actual_power, smartFloor)+1, ExcludeProvider: impl_provider_id}`.
- If Fizeau returns no-viable-provider, drop ExcludeProvider, run same-provider, emit `kind:review-pairing-degraded`.
- Remove the `reviewHarness = implHarness` default in `execute_bead_review.go:521`.

### R5 — Always review
- `--no-review` requires `--no-review-i-know-what-im-doing` to be accepted; otherwise rejected with error.
- `review:skip` label requires a sibling `review:skip-reason:<text>` label, else loop ignores the skip and runs review with a `kind:review-skip-rejected` event.
- All skip paths emit `kind:review-skipped` with rationale.

## Spec / artifact changes

- **FEAT-006**: codify "DDx escalation = MinPower bump"; document review-pairing exclude-provider need; cost-cap must include review cost.
- **FEAT-010**: state automatic-by-default invocation contract.
- **FEAT-014**: ReviewResult must carry `CostUSD`/`InputTokens`/`OutputTokens`; cost-cap accumulator includes review.
- **FEAT-019**: reviewer outputs are comparison records.
- **New ADR-021**: "Cost-tier escalation policy" — three policy labels + MinPower floors + provider-exclusion review.
- **CONTRACT-003 amendment** (upstream agentlib): add `ExcludeProvider` field to ExecuteRequest; add `ActualPower`, `ProviderID`, `ProviderClass` to ExecuteResponse; add `CostUSD`/tokens to review path.

## Implementation surface

1. `cli/internal/escalation/ladder.go`: pure `Ladder.Next(currentMinPower, status, detail) (nextMinPower int, action {Bump, Defer, Stop})`. No tier-string output; no profile output.
2. Refactor `agent_cmd.go` executor closure: replace one-shot `singleTierAttempt` with loop driven by `Ladder.Next`. Inputs to Fizeau: only MinPower (and operator-pinned harness/model/provider when present).
3. Replace `singleTierAttempt`'s `attemptProfile = TierToProfile(tier)` plumbing — no longer needed; tier label only drives initial MinPower floor.
4. `pickReviewerHarness` deleted; replaced by `BuildReviewExecuteRequest(implResult)` constructing the Fizeau request with MinPower floor + `ExcludeProvider`.
5. Add `provider_id` / `provider_class` / `actual_power` capture from Fizeau ExecuteResponse into ExecuteBeadReport, then into ReviewBead.
6. Reviewer cost: charge `costCap.Add(review.Harness, review.CostUSD)` after review dispatch.
7. `--no-review` ack flag + label rationale enforcement in `runAgentExecuteLoopImpl` and `runWork`.
8. Step 0 prompt: heighten on `len(desc+ac) > 8000`.
9. Routinglint-style guard: forbid `run/try/work` paths from setting Profile/Harness/Model unless operator-pinned.

## Acceptance Criteria

- AC1: Zero-flag `ddx work` drains a mixed-tier queue; Fizeau receives only MinPower (no harness/model/provider) per attempt unless operator pinned.
- AC2: Infrastructure failure (502, network) defers with retry-after; MinPower unchanged. Substantive failure bumps MinPower to next floor.
- AC3: Reviewer ExecuteRequest carries `ExcludeProvider = impl provider_id` and `MinPower > impl actual_power`; reviewer provider differs from implementer in 100% of cases when ≥2 providers exist.
- AC4: With one provider only, reviewer falls back to same-provider AND emits `kind:review-pairing-degraded`.
- AC5: Cost cap counts review cost; primary-success that overshoots cap on review either skips review (with `kind:review-cost-deferred` event) or applies cap policy explicitly.
- AC6: `--no-review` without ack flag returns CLI error. `review:skip` without rationale label is ignored.
- AC7: Bead description+AC > 8000 bytes triggers heightened Step 0 hint in prompt template.
- AC8: Drain never bottoms at "no viable provider": skipped tiers escalate MinPower until top-power; only true total-absence terminates with explicit error.
- AC9: Review path appends events / closes / reopens bead only; never rewrites the attempt commit chain or `closing_commit_sha`.
- AC10: `escalation-summary` event captures all attempted MinPower steps with cost and winning step (or "exhausted").

## Tests

- Unit: `TestLadderNext` table-driven (status, detail, currentPower) → (action, nextPower).
- Unit: `TestBuildReviewExecuteRequest` — exclude-provider + MinPower floor.
- Integration: `TestExecuteLoopBumpsMinPowerOnSubstantiveFailure` — mock Fizeau succeeding only at MinPower ≥ 30.
- Integration: `TestReviewerExcludesImplementerProvider` — two providers; verify ExecuteRequest field.
- Integration: `TestReviewerDegradedSingleProvider` — one provider; verify event.
- Integration: `TestReviewCostCounted` — cost cap trips on review.
- Integration: `TestNoReviewRequiresAck` — flag without ack → error.
- E2E: `TestZeroConfigDrainEndToEnd` — `ddx work --once` against fixture.
- Routinglint: regression on `run/try/work` not setting Profile/Harness/Model.

## Risks

- **Fizeau contract change**: blocking dependency. Without `ExcludeProvider` in ExecuteRequest and provider/power in ExecuteResponse, R4 cannot ship correctly. Sequence: land Fizeau extension first, then DDx side.
- **Cost spike**: review on every bead. Mitigation: cost cap counts review, plus `review-cost-deferred` policy.
- **Reviewer disagreement loop**: existing `review_max_retries` gate.
- **Decomposition cascade**: depth-2 cap.
- **Endpoint-first redesign**: this plan is endpoint-first compatible by construction (MinPower-only).

## Bead Breakdown (Epic — 9 children)

- **B1** [tier:smart] Upstream Fizeau contract extension (CONTRACT-003): `ExcludeProvider` on ExecuteRequest; `ProviderID`, `ProviderClass`, `ActualPower` on ExecuteResponse; review cost reporting. **Blocks B3, B5, B6.**
- **B2** [tier:standard] `escalation/ladder.go` (Next, MinPower-only output) + tests. Deps: none.
- **B3** [tier:standard] Wire ladder into `agent_cmd.go` executor closure (replace one-shot with loop, drop profile/tier→profile mutation). Deps: B1, B2.
- **B4** [tier:cheap] Routinglint guard against Profile/Harness/Model in run/try/work. Deps: B3.
- **B5** [tier:standard] `BuildReviewExecuteRequest` + remove implHarness default; emit `review-pairing-degraded`. Deps: B1.
- **B6** [tier:cheap] Charge reviewer cost to cost cap; `review-cost-deferred` policy + tests. Deps: B1.
- **B7** [tier:cheap] `--no-review` ack flag; `review:skip` rationale label enforcement; `review-skipped` event. Deps: none.
- **B8** [tier:cheap] Step 0 heightened hint at >8000 bytes; depth-2 decomposition cap label. Deps: none.
- **B9** [tier:smart] Update FEAT-006 / FEAT-010 / FEAT-014; write ADR-021. Deps: B1–B8.

## Open Ambiguities (final)

1. Initial MinPower floor numbers (10/30/50?) — needs Fizeau power-scale calibration.
2. Cost-cap policy when review would overshoot: skip review vs defer vs accept overshoot once. Leaning **defer** with explicit event so the bead does not silently lose its review.
3. `review:skip` rationale label format — `review:skip-reason:<text>` vs free-form note. Leaning structured label.
4. Whether `ddx run` (atom) should auto-route at all, or remain explicit since it lacks bead metadata. Leaning explicit (no auto for atom).

## Coupling to endpoint-first redesign

**Story 10 should ship in tight parallel with Fizeau endpoint-first.** B1 is the explicit Fizeau contract bead that aligns the two efforts. Implementing R4 in DDx without the Fizeau extension would create exactly the harness/profile-routing debt endpoint-first is removing.

data.text: This is an epic whose AC is "All child beads closed" — 8 of 9 children remain open. The epic itself has no implementation; it will close once its children do. Writing a no_changes rationale.

input.content:
Epic ddx-e34994e2 (Story 10: automatic escalation + always-review) has no implementation work of its own. Its acceptance criterion is literally "All child beads closed."

Current child status (parent=ddx-e34994e2):
  closed: ddx-8f1ac866 (fizeau CONTRACT-003 extension)
  open:   ddx-51ac997b (escalation ladder.Next + tests)
  open:   ddx-cfedee8e (wire ladder into executor closure)
  open:   ddx-2436fa73 (BuildReviewExecuteRequest + remove implHarness default)
  open:   ddx-a8718bec (charge reviewer cost to cap)
  open:   ddx-86996834 (--no-review break-glass + review:skip rationale)
  open:   ddx-e0be88f6 (Step 0 heightened hint + depth-2 cap)
  open:   ddx-0ca4ffda (routinglint guard)
  open:   ddx-5f1eac4f (FEAT-006/010/014 + ADR-021)

What is done: 1/9 child beads closed (the upstream Fizeau contract bead, which unblocks several others).

What is blocking: 8 child beads remain open. Per the planning round's locked decisions, those children carry the testable ACs; the epic is a tracker, not a unit of implementation. Bundling all of them into one attempt would violate the size-check guidance in the execute-bead instructions (multiple feature-sized pieces, >500 LOC across unrelated packages, AC mixes design/impl/tests/docs).

What a follow-up attempt would need: nothing at the epic level. The execute-loop should drain the children individually (most are tier:cheap or tier:standard; ddx-cfedee8e and ddx-2436fa73 unblocked by the closed B1, so they are ready). Once all children close, this epic can be closed administratively (or by re-running execute-bead, which will then find AC satisfied).

No code changes committed in this attempt.

data.text: Epic ddx-e34994e2 has no work of its own — AC is "All child beads closed," and 8 of 9 children are still open. Wrote no_changes_rationale.txt explaining the status; the loop should drain the ready children (ddx-cfedee8e and ddx-2436fa73 are now unblocked by the closed B1).
